category_title: High school student with AD/HD showing inattention and hyperactivity in traditional settings but improved engagement with ICT
student_description: M is a shy student with AD/HD who frequently daydreams and makes careless mistakes during passive, theory-heavy instruction. He uniquely requires highly visual, interactive learning tools (like simulations) to maintain focus, and needs to be prompted to answer first in group settings to prevent him from passively agreeing with peers.

criteria.rationale: M's AD/HD requires a dynamic, high-stimulus environment over time to prevent him from losing interest in the course material.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan explicitly outlines a rotation of diverse teaching methods that consistently integrate Information and Communication Technology (ICT) tools to sustain his interest.
